corrections.description: Article file contains wrong content - subscription info and hospital story instead of the poem
corrections.text:
THE STORYTELLER

Running, laughing, the happy children came
To gather 'round their grandmother's chair,
Their eyes bright with anticipation
As she smoothed back each tousled hair.

"Tell us a story, Grandma, please,
About the olden days," they'd say.
And she would smile and settle back
To tell of long-ago and far-away.

Her voice was soft and gentle
As she wove her magic spell,
Of princes brave and maidens fair,
And dragons fierce as well.

The children listened, spellbound,
To every word she'd say,
Until their eyes grew heavy
At the close of day.

Peggy Tangren
